QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_23

question.text: Mr. Sanjay has the following incomes and losses in FY 2025-26: Non-speculative business income: Rs.4,00,000; Speculative business loss: Rs.1,50,000; Loss from card games (gambling): Rs.80,000; Income from horse racing: Rs.60,000. What is Sanjay's gross total income after permissible set-offs?

answer.text: Rs.2,30,000 — all losses set off against business income and horse racing income
Rs.4,60,000 — only speculative loss set off against business income
Rs.4,60,000 — speculative loss cannot be set off against non-speculative income; gambling loss cannot be set off against any income including horse racing; GTI = Rs.4,00,000 + Rs.60,000
Rs.3,80,000 — gambling loss set off against horse racing income; speculative loss carried forward

explanation.text: **The Logic:** (1) Speculative business loss can ONLY be set off against speculative profit — not against non-speculative business income. Carried forward for 4 years. (2) Loss from gambling/card games CANNOT be set off against any other income, not even other gambling/lottery/horse racing income. Each type of Section 115BB income is treated separately, and losses from them are completely disallowed. GTI = Rs.4,00,000 (business) + Rs.60,000 (horse racing) = Rs.4,60,000. **The Trap:** Option D wrongly assumes gambling loss can be set off against horse racing income — both are taxed under Section 115BB but losses from such activities are NOT allowed to be set off at all. **Cross-Exam Context:** The absolute prohibition on gambling loss set-off is tested across NISM tax-related modules. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Income from gambling, lotteries, and horse racing is taxed at a flat 30% with no deductions or loss set-off allowed. Advise clients that these are one-way streets for taxation.
